data.title: Price prediction for WLD aims for $2.20 breakout in 2 weeks, signaling a 44% potential upside. Bearish scenarios suggest $1.40 retest if support fails.
data.description: According to recent analysis, there is a potential 44% increase in the WLD price, which could reach a resistance level of $2.20 in the next two weeks. However, if the current support level does not hold, bearish outcomes indicate a possible retest at $1.40.

data.title: "Tour guide app Le Walk raises $4.1M in seed funding for global expansion and new features by 2026"
data.description: Le Walk, a prominent location-based guided experiences app, has announced a plan for global expansion, feature enhancements, and the introduction of new premium offerings by 2026. This initiative is being supported by top venture capitalists, with a $4.1 million seed funding round led by Adverb. The company made its public debut in New York on September 19, 2025.

data.title: Austria construction industry to slightly recover by 0.8% in 2025 after 3 years of contraction. Growth forecasted until 2029.
data.description: In Dublin, a recent report titled "Austria Construction Market Size, Trends, and Forecasts by Sector" has been incorporated into ResearchAndMarkets.com's collection. After a period of three years marked by decline, the Austrian construction sector is now displaying indications of improvement, with an anticipated growth of 0.8% in real terms for 2025. This recovery is bolstered by declining interest rates. The report provides in-depth analysis across various sectors, including Commercial, Industrial, Infrastructure, Energy and Utilities, Institutional, and Residential markets, up to 2029.

data.title: Price prediction: Target at $4.15 in 2 weeks due to bullish momentum, with medium-term range of $4.20-$9.10.
data.description: Short-term price forecasts for SUI project a target of $4.15, backed by bullish momentum in technical indicators. Looking ahead, medium-term predictions suggest a range of $4.20 to $9.10 for Sui.

data.title: ISX Financial renews sponsorship with APOEL FC through 2025-2027 seasons in Nicosia, providing banking and payments tech support.
data.description: Today in Nicosia, Cyprus, ISX Financial EU Plc (ISXX) revealed the extension of its sponsorship deal with APOEL FC, a renowned football club. The company, a regulated Electronic Money Institution, offers transactional banking and real-time payments technology in Europe and the UK. This partnership renewal underscores their commitment to supporting APOEL FC.

data.title: FedEx beats Q1 revenue estimates, reporting $22.2 billion vs $21.67 billion. Adjusted earnings soar to $3.83 per share.QtCoreFedEx projects 4-6% revenue growth for fiscal 2026.
data.description: FedEx Corporation (NYSE: FDX) exceeded expectations in its first-quarter fiscal 2026 financial results. The company announced a revenue of $22.2 billion, surpassing analyst projections of $21.67 billion. Adjusted earnings for the quarter were $3.83 per share, beating estimates of $3.62 per share. With a daily package movement of 17 million through its network, CEO Raj Subramaniam attributed the success to strategic initiatives and operational capabilities. Moving forward, FedEx anticipates a 4-6% increase in revenue for fiscal 2026. More details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Johnson & Wales University introduces 3-year (90 credit) Hospitality Management degree in Charlotte. Apply for Fall 2026.
data.description: Johnson & Wales University (JWU) in Charlotte, North Carolina, announced on September 19, 2025, that it is accepting applications for its new three-year bachelor's degree program in Hospitality Management for the Fall 2026 term. The program, totaling 90 credits, is being offered at the university's Charlotte Campus. Interested students can now apply to be a part of this innovative program designed to equip them with the necessary skills and knowledge for a successful career in the hospitality industry.

data.title: Kimberly Wagner named AVP Product & Underwriting at Preferred Employers Insurance, a Berkley company, with 25+ years experience.
data.description: Preferred Employers Insurance, a Berkley company based in San Diego, has recently appointed Kimberly Wagner as their new Assistant Vice President of Product & Underwriting. Wagner comes with a wealth of experience in the industry, boasting over 25 years of expertise in underwriting, operations, and product development. This move is set to enhance the company's leadership team and strengthen their capabilities in these crucial areas.
